name: Reduced Launch Cost 4.1
summary: Reduces the cost for launching your ship from the ground. Options for free cost, 5% and 10% of total energy per launch
author: Lex - lo2k
description:
[size=4]Reduced Launch Cost
[/size]
[b]4.1 version is compatible with Cursed 5.29[/b]

Removes or lowers the fuel cost for launching your ship from the ground.
[b]
[/b][b][color=#ff7700]Note 1 : Each ship type uses different fuel quantities when taking-off and playing in survival doubles fuel quantities !
[b]Note 2 : Since a few game updates, you can use supercharged slots to improve fuel efficiency, get ingame technology modules reducing fuel quantity used per launch or even slowly recharging your fuel tank when landed ![/b][/color][/b]

Options for free cost (instead of 25% of energy per launch), 10% and 5% of total energy per launch

[size=3]Installation[/size]
[list]
[*]Unzip the zip file
[*]Pick the desired pak
[list]
[*] 0% cost per launch: ReducedLaunchCost_0.pak
[*]5% cost per launch: Reduced Launch Cost 5.pak
[*]10% cost per launch: Reduced Launch Cost 10.pak
[/list][*]Copy the desired pak file to No Man's Sky/GAMEDATA/MODS (Create the folder if it doesn't exist)
[*]Delete No Man's Sky/GAMEDATA/PCBANKS/DISABLEMODS.txt if it exists
[/list]

[i]Note:[/i] If the mod doesn't seem to work, you likely have a mod conflict. Try renaming this mod to be last (e.g. XXReducedLaunchCost_0.pak)
[color=#f1c232]This mod is incompatible with any other mod that modifies[/color][b] [color=#f1c232]NMS_REALITY_GCTECHNOLOGYTABLE.MBIN[/color] [/b][color=#f1c232]but a .lua file is provided for each variant if you want to merge it with any other mod written for[/color][b] [/b][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/957][color=#ffd966]AMUMSS[/color][/url][color=#f1c232]
[/color]

[size=3]Un-installation[/size]
[list]
[*]Delete the selected pak file from No Man's Sky/GAMEDATA/PCBANKS/MODS
[/list]

[size=3]Changes[/size]
File: METADATA/REALITY/TABLES/NMS_REALITY_GCTECHNOLOGYTABLE.MBIN

name: Russian Vintage Flags
summary: New outpost flags
author: RD
description:
Here's 9 new flags, Russian space race propaganda from the 50-60s.
I really like the art style in these so I brought them in-game.
They replace the standard building flags.

So, how did they ended up on an alien planet?
Backstory: In the 60's Russia launched a secret ship heading for the nearest star.
After 4 generations the descendants of the original crew landed on a planet.
(There was actually a mini-series made about this: [url=http://www.imdb.com/title/tt3696720/]Ascension[/url].)

Installation: copy to your PCBANKS folder.

Also check out my: [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/205/?]Russian style mod overhaul
[/url]

name: HD Normal-maps for LargePlant Objects
summary: Outdated - HD Replacer for LARGEPLANT objects normal maps. All biomes.
author: Virakotxa
description:
Outdated - 
 1.1 (Foundation) Compatible.
 
Enhanced normal maps, twice or triple the size of the original textures.

 The mod overwrites the normal-maps of the large plants and trees that are named as such, their actual colour, variations and any fancy code related to them is not touched by this mod. This is just a retexture... of the [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Normal_mapping]fake geometry that gets applied over the real geometry.

[/url] Note that this doesn't mean that every tree, and only that, is covered by the mod. Some small plants get also retextured, but many larger ones are not... since they use files from middle or small plants. Large fungi included, I've decided to leave out Underwater and Caves for now.

files.name: Virakotxa's TreeHugger
files.description: Enhanced HD LargePlant Normals

name: Portal glyph names and hex numerals
summary: Added names and hexadecimal numerals for glyphs
author: BFleur
description:
For better memorization and easy publishing portal addresses i've added additional info for each glyph.
You can use https://nmsportals.github.io/ for converting hexadecimal numerals into glyphs

name: Better Planet Generation
summary: A huge planet generation overhaul that aims at making your hundreds of hours of exploration in the magnificent, infinite universe of No Man's Sky less repetitive and hopefully even more memorable.
author: WoodyMontana
description:
[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=5][i][b]                                                     [size=6]Better Planet Generation[/size][/b][/i][/size]

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1629738968-1312445652.jpeg[/img]

[size=4][b]More planets

[/b][size=3]Better Planet Generation is a total overhaul of the planetary generation in No Man's Sky.[/size] [size=3]It improves by a significant margin the procedural aspect of the universe while at the same time respecting the original work and vision from Hello Games.[/size]

[size=3]It adds thousands of new distinct environments to the universe[/size][/size]. All of them come in five different life levels and varying placement scales and layouts. These environments were made to seamlessly combine with each other in many different ways.

Better Planet Generation is designed to bring variety to the different biomes you will visit but most of all it brings a lot of variation to the universe. Outside of the countless combinations of assets and their resulting new environments, planets you visit will present themselves differently each time. Placement layouts, scale of the regions, density, variety of assets and their respective scale, ...

No planet will look exactly the same. Unless, of course, it is a very simple one. More on that below.

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1631005159-472402214.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630936034-1789086190.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1610300150-1298994048.jpeg[/img]


[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=4][b]Sub Biomes[/b][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size]

Caves and underwater sub biomes have also been entirely overhauled.

Tons of new variants have been added underground and just like on the surface, additional objects can spawn to increase variety even more.

﻿[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630410276-924973523.jpeg[/img]﻿

Underwater has been completely revamped and is now generated through a triple layered dynamic framework combining assets to generate highly varied and detailed environments.

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1629738939-165524032.jpeg[/img]
[b][size=4]
Unique planets

[/size][/b][size=4][size=3]On top of all the added variety, BPG also uses a flexible dynamic generation framework that can take many shapes[/size][/size] and create countless planetary variations, even when the same environments are being used multiple times.

Each planet generated has its own selection of parts based on its biome. Some liberties have been taken when designing and attributing those parts to the various biomes. Some objects have been injected to improve variety, but some restrictions have also been applied to preserve each biome's identity. Better Planet Generation also introduces countless restored, modified or repurposed assets to the game in order to increase the variety while preserving its visual style.

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630936068-132859967.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630936034-754906087.jpeg[/img]

[/size][/size][/size][/size]
[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][b][size=4]Natural Generation[/size][/b][/size][/size][/size][/size]

Better Planet Generation is a vastly different experience compared to the unmodded game and even other biome mods.
Not even taking into account the small details, it is more than likely that you will never be able to encounter all the possible assets combinations this mod can generate. Let alone in the different regional layouts each dynamic template can result in.

Each biome has access to multiple procedural templates. Each producing different results in terms of layouts and objects combinations.
Their common goal is to show you a different kind of environment each time. Aesthetically and structurally.
No more "postcard" planets where most of what there is to see is right around the corner. The scale of the generation can get extremely vast compared to the vanilla game.

Planetary generation can now affect your visual enjoyment and the gameplay.
It is a video game, after all.

A world generated at its fullest possible level and highest complexity can take you some time to fully explore on foot. Meaning seeing the most notable regions (and not all of them) and scanning everything.

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630052272-1155900954.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1610300150-2086924238.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630272224-118529207.jpeg[/img]
[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][b][size=4]More Variation[/size][/b][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size]

On the other hand, Better Planet Generation also allows more simple, generic yet still visually interesting planets to see :

- Planets only covered in grass and some plants, rocks or both.
- More barren worlds dominated by giant stone or crystal spires.
- Radioactive wastelands.
- Planets dotted with trees or occasional small forests.
- Volcanic wastelands.
- Toxic fields.
- Rocky swamps.
- ...

And the list goes on and on.
[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3]The occurence of these "generic" planets only relies on probabilites. None of my templates is designed to specifically produce them.
I made sure they still looked good, though.[/size][/size][/size][/size]

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630410296-1590906166.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1626079315-1486130072.jpeg[/img]

[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][b][size=4]Different Experience[/size][/b][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size][/size]

Better Planet Generation is not for everyone. Planets can be both very detailed in every aspect or very simple.
If you expect to always see trees and a full grass coverage on your Lush planet or anywhere else, there are other mods for that.
This is not what you should expect using BPG.

Now, whether you are a new player ready to explore an infinite universe or a more weathered one that wants to be surprised by the game again, I suggest you give Better Planet Generation a try. It won't harm your game in any way.

[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1629923717-282604290.jpeg[/img][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/797/797-1630410307-2137550589.jpeg[/img]

[size=5][size=3][size=4][size=3][b][size=5]WARNING BEFORE INSTALLING :[/size][/b]

Installing this mod will operate changes to the terrain in every biomes.
It is also recommended to install it while in a space station / anomaly / freighter and not on a planet's surface.
Some major updates to the mod can reset the terrain or biome lists.
I'll include a warning on the download page and the sticky when it's the case.[/size][/size][/size][/size]


Recommended :
[/size][/size]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/968]DUD's Sky - Colors[/url] : load planetary colour palettes before BPG to avoid procedural textures conflicts.
[/size][/size]

name: AMUMSS (auto modbuilder-updater with MOD script definition system)
summary: Revolutionary No Man's Sky AMUMSS (auto modbuilder/updater with MOD script definition system)Fully automatic mod builder that automates every step of nms modding and provides an easy to use lua script mod definition system.
author: Mjjstral aka MetaIdea and Wbertro aka TheBossBoy
description:
[color=#ff0000][size=6]-> [/size][size=5]Looking for lua scripts, see:[/size][size=6] [url=https://github.com/MetaIdea/nms-amumss-lua-mod-script-collection]AMUMSS LUA MOD SCRIPT ARCHIVE[/url] <-[/size][/color]

[font=Verdana][color=#ffe599][size=6]AMUMSS is NOT a mod manager, nor can it be used as such.  It is a script processor.[/size][/color][/font]

[color=#ff0000][size=5]AMUMSS does NOT need updating to continue working when NMS updates.[/size]
[size=4]       When the tool MBINCompiler.exe is updated, AMUMSS will download it automatically.[/size][/color]
AMUMSS will auto-update itself (when allowed), if a new version is available (Just execute BUILDMOD.bat as usual and read simple instructions)
[list]
[*]The tools MBINCompiler/libMBIN are auto-updated by AMUMSS.[/*]
[*]Scripts are NEVER updated by AMUMSS, you are responsible for using updated scripts, when they need to be.[/*]
[/list]
[b][b][color=#ff7700][size=3]Questions will be answered if asked in NMS Discord[/size]: here[/color] [url=https://discord.gg/22ZAU9H][color=#00ff00]https://discord.gg/22ZAU9H[/color]
[/url][/b]
IMPORTANT NOTES:[/b]
[list]
[*][b]This tool works as long as MBINCompiler can compile/decompile the MBIN touched by the scripts[/b][/*]
[*][b]NEW version [color=#ff00ff]does[/color] requires [i][color=#ffff00].NET 6 Desktop Runtime x64[/color][/i] to be installed (a MBINCompiler.exe requirement)  It will attempt to auto-install.[/b][/*]
[*][b][color=#ff0000]Note:[/color] Sometimes, your anti-virus may detect some component of AMUMSS and block/quarantine it.  Be assure it is not a virus but its [i][color=#ff0000]behavior [/color][/i]may be interpreted as such by some anti-virus.  Please make sure to create an exception in your anti-virus when this happens.  Also a reboot may be required as some anti-virus do not correctly register the exception when created.[/b][/*]
[/list]
[b][i][size=3]INCLUDES:[/size][/i][/b]
[list]
[*][b][i][size=3][b][i][size=3]NMSPE [/size][/i][/b]([u][b]NMS PCBANKS Explorer)[/b][/u] a [color=#ff0000][b]GUI app to explore libMBIN.dll, NMS PAKS and Unpack/Decompile/Import-Search EXML content[/b][/color][/size][/i][/b][/*]
[*][b][i][size=3][b][i][size=3]AMM [/size][/i][/b]([u][b]_ModScript_Manager)[/b][/u] a [color=#ff0000][b]GUI app simplifying usage of scripts in ModScript folder[/b][/color][/size][/i][/b][/*]
[*][b][i][size=3][b][i][size=3]MFM [/size][/i][/b]([u][b]_MapFileMaker)[/b][/u]: a [color=#ff0000][b]NEW console app to create MapFileTree files from EXMLs using drag/drop[/b][/color][/size][/i][/b][/*]
[/list][b]
[b][color=#ff0000][size=4]Installation: [/size][/color][/b][/b]
[list]
[*][b][size=3][color=#00ff00]See [/color][color=#00ff00][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626][/url][/color][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626]https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626[/url]﻿ [color=#00ff00]for detailed instructions![/color][/size][/b]
[b]* [color=#ffff00]Get the latest release from[/color] [url=https://github.com/HolterPhylo/AMUMSS/releases]AMUMSS latest releases on Github Link
[/url]* De-compress the zip file to its own folder (like C:\AMUMSS)   Making the path to AMUMSS main folder short helps prevent bumping against windows path length limit (in rare cases)
* No accented or special characters in the path
* Always extract to a new folder on any drive, never in any System, Documents or game folder
* Create an exception to AMUMSS main folder BEFORE executing anything
[/b][color=#ffff00][b]* If the cmd windows closes immediately (or is blank) when double-clicking BUILDMOD.BAT:
          if you are on windows 7.  Try making '-UseColors N' in BUILDMOD_AUTO.bat and use it instead of BUILDMOD.bat

[/b][/color][/*]
[/list][b]Amumss lua related support: [url=https://discord.gg/22ZAU9H]NMS Modding Discord[/url]? - see #amumss-lua channel
[/b]

name: Fast Actions 7.1
summary: Faster clicks and key pressed actions
author: Lo2k
description:
Instead of pressing a key for almost one second, this mod provides quicker or instant inputs for :
- menu selection
- creating/dismantling technology
- transfering resources to/from ship
- entering ship
- discussing with NPC
- gathering small plants
- launching refinery processing
- recovering deployable technologies

Enjoy !
[url=http://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/28/?]
[/url]This mod exists in 2 versions :
﻿- instant actions : almost no delay actions (Fractal 4.13 added an in-game option but it only works for a limited range of actions)
- fast actions : between original and instant actions

[color=#ffd966]New : v7.1 fixes precise item quantity amount selection with Instant Actions. (Fast Action is untouched)[/color]


[size=2]Compatibility : [/size]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/880][color=#ffd966]- [u]CHECK IF THIS MOD IS COMPATIBLE WITH LATEST GAME VERSION[/u][/color][/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/articles/85][color=#f6b26b]- [u]CHECK IF THIS MOD NEEDS TO BE MERGED WITH ANOTHER OF MY MODS[/u][/color][/url]

Credits : Many thanks to Shadwar for [b]the original version[/b]

name: Exosolar's and Babs' Beyond Base Building
summary: ★ FULLY MP compatible, this mod adds an extreme degree of freedom to the vanilla base-building options
author: Exosolar Sparky NeptuneX3 Babscoole
description:
[b][color=#f1c232][size=4]What is this Mod?[/size][/color][/b]
[size=4]There are now almost 1,000 buildable base parts in vanilla No Man's Sky! It's a dream come true!
This mod adds an extreme degree of freedom to the vanilla base-building options.
This does NOT mean the mod gives you access to all buildable parts.  You still need to learn the blueprints for each.  :)
Beyond Base Building does NOT add any new build parts to the game.

Now includes all the functionality of gBase Items, [color=#ff0000]if chosen by running the Lua script[/color]:[/size]

[list]
[*][size=4]Can multiply power, extraction, and storage rates by 2, 5, or 10. WARNING: Bases built with this mod cannot be uploaded for multiplayer when using 2x, 5x, or 10x multiplied rates.[/size][/*]
[/list]
[list]
[*][size=4]EM Generators do not need a hotspot and produce 250 power.  Not enabled by default.  Must run Lua through ANUMSS and answer question "Choose a multiplier for Storage and Rates and EMAnywhere status" to generate custom mod file to enable.[/size][/*]
[/list][size=4]

It is [b]fully compatible[/b] with multiplayer, co-op building, uploading, etc, because it [i]does not add[/i] any parts, which would cause issues in multiplayer.[/size]

[size=4][color=#f1c232][b]★ Beyond Basebuilding[/b] is fully Lua scripted, with script provided for easy updating and merging [b]★[/b][/color] and carries over all of the official updates and improvements offered by Hello Games [b]plus[/b]:[/size]

[list]
[*][size=4]Most parts can be Scaled [i][color=#fff2cc]except[/color][/i] prefab building parts, [color=#fff2cc]Exocraft Geobays, Mineral/Gas Extractors and Base Computer[/color] (Door attachment points bug out when scaled, and issues persist with scaled base computers and geobays).  There may be others not listed.  Be aware, scaling breaks snapping.[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Many parts can be built in a Freighter.  [color=#fff2cc][i]Exceptions[/i][/color]  include the Base Computer, Industrial Mining parts, Vehicle Summoners and planet-based teleporter.[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Many parts can be built at a Planetary Base [i][color=#fff2cc]except[/color][/i] the freighter teleporter and the Orbital Exocraft Materialiser[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]All parts can be built above and below water[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]All parts can change color and material[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]All parts, which can be deleted, can be deleted without also deleting attached objects [color=#fff2cc][i]except[/i][/color] Decals and Posters[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]No build-count limits [i][color=#fff2cc]except[/color][/i] for parts related to resources harvesting, certain terminals, beacons and message capsules (and others with limits that are hard coded into the game, as they are added or changed by Hello Games).[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Unlimited Storage Containers[color=#cccccc][size=3][i]—*This does not increase actual storage amounts: Each numbered container acts as an index to the store rather than a physical box[/i][/size][/color][/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Foundation, Pavings and Communications Station can be placed anywhere (not only on bare terrain)[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Non corvette parts buildable with corvettes[/size][/*]
[/list][size=4]
Some customization of the mod is available just by running it through AMUMSS, where you'll be prompted about enabling several feature.  Even more customization can be had by editing the top portion of the Lua before processing.  See Lua usage below for link to AMUMSS install and basic usage How to.[/size]

[color=#f1c232][b][u][size=5]Disclaimer:[/size][/u][/b][/color]
[size=3]This MOD makes changes to the placement and usage setting of vanilla parts.  If the MOD is removed, the vanilla settings will be restored and parts that "violate" a vanilla setting as well as those connected may go "poof".  Best practice is to regularly backup your saves for semi-painless recovery from the unexpected.[/size]

[center][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/images/1634/95741288-1598421367.png[/img][/center]

[size=5][b][u][color=#f1c232]Installation[/color]
[/u][/b][/size]
[b]Extract archive contents to GAMEDATA\MODS

[color=#f1c232][size=4][u]Lua usage[/u][/size][/color]
[size=4][b]✦[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626]How to use AMUMSS at No Man's Sky Nexus - Mods and Community (nexusmods.com)[/url]✦[/b][/size][/b]

[size=4][u][b][color=#f1c232]Modified Files[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[color=#d8d8d8][size=3]METADATA\REALITY\TABLES\BASEBUILDINGOBJECTSTABLE.MBIN[/size][/color]

[i][color=#cccccc][size=3]Also check out...[/size][/color][/i]
[size=5]✦[b][color=#ffdd00][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/users/58018181?tab=user+files]Exosolar's Other Mods[/url][/color][/b]✦[/size]


[center][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/images/1634/95741288-1598421367.png[/img][/center]

[size=5][u][b][color=#f1c232]Is this Mod up-to-date?[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[size=4][i][b]Any mod files which are not hidden or demoted to the "Old Files" area can be expected to work.[/b][/i]
All uploaded files (on the Files page) are maintained with their packing date and version of the MBINCompiler used to pack it. Older mod versions, no longer compatible with the current version of No Man's Sky will be demoted to the "Old Files" subsection, but will remain available for download. There are no side-effects to using this mod.[/size]

[size=4][u][b][color=#f1c232]How to update, tweak, combine, or patch the mod with AMUMSS?[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[size=4]As this mod is now Lua scripted, you can [color=#fff2cc]easily [b]update[/b], [b]tweak[/b] or [b]merge[/b] the mod yourself[/color] (if a game update breaks this PAK of the mod, or you have other PAK or Lua mods that could conflict with this one). [color=#cccccc][size=3][i]—*Click on the spoiler below for more[/i][/size][/color][/size]
[spoiler]
[size=3][u]Notes on AMUMSS:[/u]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/957]AMUMSS[/url] tries to update [url=https://github.com/monkeyman192/MBINCompiler/releases]MBINcompiler[/url] automatically. If it fails to convert EXML/MBIN files, its likely due to a very recent NMS version update and MBINcompiler is awaiting an update too.
If so, I highly recommend you to disable your mods and wait for MBINCompiler to be updated (it's pretty quick in general).[/size]


[size=3][u][b][color=#f1c232]Updating the mod after a game update:[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[spoiler][list]
[*] [size=3]Download [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/957]AMUMSS[/url], and extract it locally,  avoid placing the AMUMSS folder in a path with special characters or in a system folder like [color=#fff2cc][i]"C:\Program Files (x86)\(...)"[/i][/color].[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Place the mod Lua script (only the .LUA file, not the .PAK file) in the [color=#fff2cc][i]ModScript[/i][/color] folder of AMUMSS.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]AMUMSS will normally auto-detect the NMS installation folder, but you can also manually edit the path in the [color=#fff2cc][i]NMS_FOLDER.txt[/i][/color] file.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Launch [color=#fff2cc][i]BUILDMOD.bat[/i][/color] in the root of the AMUMSS folder and follow the steps, selecting the option to automatically install it in the NMS [color=#fff2cc][i]MODS[/i][/color] folder.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]A new modded PAK file will be created, using the last update of the MBIN game files duly amended by the script.[/size][/*]
[/list]
[size=3]Most of the time simply recompiling the mod using the Lua script will make it compatible again. Use the latest script to ensure you adopt any mod adjustments to accommodate the latest NMS build.[/size][size=3] There are two main causes of mods not correctly updating with this method:
1) MBINCompiler needs an update to be able to decompile the latest version of the game MBIN files.
2) Structural changes have been made to the MBIN files called by this Lua script. Whilst unlikely, this will require some script rewrite and other mods are likely affected.[/size][/spoiler]

[size=3][u][b][color=#f1c232]Tweaking the mod:[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[spoiler][size=3]Use the same process as described in [color=#fff2cc][i]Updating the mod[/i][/color].
Just modify the script as you want before recompiling it. Look at the top of my scripts, you can generally edit modifiers there, or even disable/enable some features very easily.[/size]

[size=3][color=#cccccc][i]*[url=https://notepad-plus-plus.org/]Notepad++[/url] or [url=https://code.visualstudio.com/download/]VSCode[/url] are generally recommended to read EXML and Lua files, due to large file support and automatic highlighting. The [url=https://sourceforge.net/projects/npp-compare/]Compare plugin[/url] for Notepad++, and Lua is also a must-have to highlight file differences.[/i][/color][/size][/spoiler]

[size=3][u][b][color=#f1c232]Combining mods that provide Lua scripts:[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[spoiler][size=3]You'll want to do this if multiple mods affect the same MBIN file.[/size]

[list]
[*] [size=3]Place all the scripts in the [color=#fff2cc][i]ModScript[/i][/color] folder of AMUMSS.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Launch [color=#fff2cc][i]BUILDMOD.bat[/i][/color] in the root folder of AMUMSS.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Follow the step, selecting that you want to combine all the scripts into one PAK file, and the option to automatically install it in the NMS [color=#fff2cc][i]MODS[/i][/color] folder.[/size][/*]
[/list]
[size=3]AMUMSS will output a PAK file that will contain the modifications of all the scripts.[/size]
[color=#cccccc][size=3][i]*If the scripts modify the exact same values in the MBIN file, the last loaded script will overwrite the preceding script values.[/i][/size][/color][/spoiler]

[size=3][u][b][color=#f1c232]Patch in the mod to make it compatible with other PAK files:[/color][/b][/u][/size]
[spoiler][size=3]You will want to do this if another mods isn't Lua scripted, and affects the same MBIN file as the Lua scripted mod.

It will work when :
Case 1) you patch a Lua scripted mod for only one other PAK.
Case 2) you patch a Lua scripted mod for several other PAKs, but only if the other PAKs don't modify the same MBIN files between them.[/size]

[size=3][b]How to create a patched PAK:[/b][/size]

[list]
[*] [size=3]Copy the script and the other mod(s) PAK(s) in the [color=#fff2cc][i]ModScript[/i][/color] folder of AMUMSS.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Launch [color=#fff2cc][i]BUILDMOD.bat[/i][/color] in the root folder of AMUMSS, and follow the steps, selecting the option to automatically install it in the NMS [color=#fff2cc][i]MODS[/i][/color] folder.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]The new PAK file will contain all the Lua script modifications, and a compatibility patch for the other mod(s) PAK(s).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Rename it something like [color=#fff2cc][i]"__zzz Scripted Mod Name -Patched For- The Other Mod Name.pak"[/i][/color].[/size]
[size=3][color=#fff2cc][i]"__zzz"[/i][/color] prefix is to ensure that it'll be loaded after the mod(s) it was patched for (for NMS loading order, underscore [i]"_"[/i] came after every other characters).[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]Remove the original scripted mod PAK from the [color=#fff2cc][i]MODS[/i][/color] folder, and copy both the new patched one, and the other mod(s) PAK(s), into it.[/size][/*]
[/list][size=3][u]Notes:[/u]
[b]Be sure the mod PAK you want to create a compatibility patch for, is updated for the latest NMS version.[/b]
-> When AMUSS find an MBIN file affected by a script and by a PAK in the [color=#fff2cc][i]ModScript[/i][/color] folder, it will take the MBIN file from the mod PAK over the original one of the game, and will apply to it the modifications listed in the script.
So, if the MBIN file of the mod PAK is not updated, the one in the resulting patched PAK won't be either.[/size][/spoiler]
[/spoiler]

[center][img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/images/1634/95741288-1598421367.png[/img][/center]
[color=#f1c232][u][b][size=5]Philosophy[/size][/b][/u][/color]
[size=4]All the erroneous limitations in building are pretty silly, aren't they? In making this mod, I paid specific attention to making sure I did not make changes that will invalidate the bases built with it from online play. Any base made with Beyond BaseBuilding will be fully viable and visible in online play, even when other players don't have this mod! It does this by only editing placement rules while you are in the building editor and not modifying any of the actual base parts themselves.
We hope you enjoy the mod and naturally, your comments are most welcome. We can also be found on the No Man's Sky Discord or reached directly at: [color=#f1c232]Babscoole #6326 [/color]or [color=#f1c232]Exosolar#5120[/color], or if its about the Lua script: [color=#f1c232]NeptuneX3#8095[/color] [size=3][i][size=4]or [/size][/i][/size][color=#f1c232]s p a r k y#0536[/color][/size]

files.changelog_html: Remove base build limit for wonder projector.

3.73: Updated to 3.73.  Some minor changes to the LUA for compatibility with AMUMSS 3.9.5.X+ syntax checking
3.81: Updated to 3.81.  Using a non-public nightly release of MBINCompiler, so fingers crossed.  :)
3.82: Update to 3.82.  Hopefully fixed the color issue, as all platforms are using the same game file version.
3.97: Update for 3.97.  First swing at Endurance compatibility.  Lots of stuff left to go over, so a work in progress
3.98: Update for 3.98.  Planter boxes now available on freighters.  Removed build limits to datasign.
3.99: Lots of Endurance "fixes".  GeoBays buildable on Freighters again (Change GEOBAYS_ON_FREIGHTER to true in LUA and run through AMUMSS, false by default).  Added back many other items by default as well.  Farm plants plantable in any biome.
4.03: Change to the LUA script only for use with AMUMSS 4.0.0.5.  Will be prompted in AMUMSS if you want GeoBays on freighters.  No changes to the mod itself
4.06: Update for 4.06.  Added Base storage containers to freighters, off by default.
4.08: Added FREIGHTERROOMS_ON_PLANETBASE.  By default disabled.  To enable, run the lua script through AMUMSS.  Say yes to enable global input.  When prompted, say Y to enable "Freighter Tech and Bio rooms on Planet Bases".  Overwrite old .pak with the new one generated.
4.38: Changes to the Lua ONLY.  Made some changes to prevent warnings/notices in AMUMSS when combining/merging with certain other base building mods.  Renamed the script to change AMUMSS processing order when combining/merging with certain other base building mods that could lead to a game crash.
4.45a: Removed planet build limits for Firepit and Spawner Ball
4.62: Update for Orbital.  Incorporates some changes from gBase Items
4.65a: MARINESHELTER_ABOVE_WATER now true by default.  Combined EMAnywhere and Multiplier selection into one prompt in the Lua
4.65b: Fixed typos in the multiplier prompt in the Lua script.  Geobays should now be scalable by default.  Fixed some notices when certain optional features were enabled via prompt when Lua is run.
4.72: Add Base Salvage Capsules on Freighters, add optional NPCSTATION_ON_FREIGHTER
5.26: Update for NMS 5.26.  Increase number of landing pads per planet base by 25%
5.28: Update for NMS 5.28.  Increase power range in freighters
5.28a: 2nd attempt at increasing free power range.  1st broke airlock door working, so reverting and trying from Bridge Connector.
5.29: Add anomaly seal (S16_HEATER) to freighter build
6.00: Compatibility for NMS 6.00 update.  Thee have been no changes dealing with corvette parts.  I'll look into that in coming days.
6.01: Hopefully added all parts not available in corvettes to corvettes.  I fully expect this to need to be dialed back a bit.
6.03: Fixes issue with double Short Range Teleporters
6.04: Update for NMS 6.04.  Added AMUMSS prompt to place Corvette parts on Planet Bases to script, so don't need to manually edit the script.  Default is Y
6.06a: Correct old typos in Lua script
6.23: Fixed a labelling error in the Lua script
6.33: Resolved issue with freighter tech rooms not showing up for some people in corvettes
6.34: Remove base build limit for wonder projector.

name: Asteroid Reduction -- PRISMS
summary: Drastically reduces asteroid density and increased draw distances.
author: HennySea
description:
[b]UPDATED FOR PRISMS[/b]

Some quick and simple edits to decrease the density of asteroids, as well as increasing their draw distances so they no longer appear to materialize out of the ether. 

- Spacing between asteroids increased
- Draw distances of asteroids also drastically increased
- Minimum distance from planets pushed out to 50,000 units (was 10,000)
- Asteroids can now surround stations
- Resource drops from asteroids increased to compensate


Not compatible with any other mod which edits /GCSOLARGENERATIONGLOBALS.GLOBAL.MBIN.

name: Less AI Ships
summary: This Mod removes all or some of the AI ships noisily flying in the planet's atmosphere or turning forever above outposts.
author: Frahel
description:
This Mod removes all or some of the AI ships noisily flying in the planet's atmosphere or turning forever above outposts.

This is a revival of Kamosabe's "No Random AI Ships on Planets" Mod, working with the latest version of No Man's Sky: Beyond.

There are two versions of this mod, please use only one:
[list=1]
[*][code]Mod_NoAIShips.pak[/code] removes all AI ships on planets.
[*][code]Mod_SomeAIShips.pak[/code] removes all AI ships except around trading posts.
[/list]
This Mod does not affect ships in space, interstellar hubs or the Nexus.

[b]Installation[/b]

[list=1]
[*]Navigate to No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\PCBANKS.
[*]Delete or rename the DISABLEMODS.TXT file.
[*]Create a folder named MODS if it does not already exists.
[*]Unzip the archive and copy one of the two .pak files to the MODS folder.
[/list]
[b]Disclaimer[/b]

This Mod may conflict with other Mods modifying the [code]EXPERIENCESPAWNTABLE.MBIN[/code] file.

name: Better Rewards
summary: Increase the amount of currency you get with faster word learning and increased resource gathering options. Frigate (Freighter) Expeditions are also included!
author: MrTrack
description:
Better Rewards increases the amount of units, nanite clusters and quicksilver you get from completing missions or interacting with objects in the world. Non npc word learning (such as knowledge stones and dictionaries) is also faster.

[color=#bf9000][size=3]IMPORTANT NOTE: Don't use this mod when I haven't updated it to the new game version (especially when it is a new or returning expedition)
[/size][/color][color=#bf9000]if you really want to use it when I haven't updated it yet, make sure you [/color][color=#bf9000]back up your save[/color][color=#bf9000] or build the mod using the lua scripts yourself.[/color]

[b]Currency[/b]

Currency gain is roughly increased by 5 times, so as to make it a viable option besides crafting and selling valuable trading goods. Some unit values were tremendously increased (eg.: from minimum amount of 1000-2000 to 20000-50000) like the terminals in abandoned settlements to make the early game less grindy.

Includes:
[list]
[*]Space station missions and Nexus missions
[*]Interactable objects/devices (abandoned buildings, damaged machinery, waypoints, etc.)
[*]Sentinels
[*]Alien interactions
[/list]
[b]Words[/b]

Learning words from non npc interactions (knowledge stones and dictionaries) is also 3 times faster (3 words learned form a single knowledge stone). You now also learn 5 Atlas words, instead of 1 and also have a chance (15%) to learn them when interacting with non npc learning sources.

[b]Resources

[/b]When gathering with the mining beam you generally get 2 times more resources (but only when the things explode as you mine them). Also includes containers, depots, guild rewards, asteroids and ships that drop resources.

[b]Frigate (Freighter) Expedition Rewards

[/b]After a successful expedition, when collecting your rewards you get 5x more units and nanites, 2x more products like Storage Augmentation and NipNip Buds and 3x more resources like Copper, Emeril and Cadmium.
[b]
Installation

[/b]Make sure you delete or edit DISABLEMODS.TXT in your game library (...\Steam\steamapps\common\No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\PCBANKS) before trying to use mods. Extract the zip file to ...\Steam\steamapps\common\No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\MODS folder and you are good to go (Create MODS folder if you don't have one). Alternatively you can just use Vortex to download and install.

This mod modifies the [color=#ff0000][size=3]REWARDTABLE.MBIN[/size][/color] and [color=#ff0000][size=3]EXPEDITIONREWARDTABLE.MBIN[/size][/color] file and will not work with other mods that use those tables.

[b]Updated to NMS version 6.21 (Build ID: 21918502) on 2026.02.13.[/b]

name: Kibbles 'n Bits
summary: A custom base building mod that adds several custom base parts and QOL tweaks to overall building mechanics. This mod contains all of my base parts and building tweaks that I had in Eucli-ea previously.
author: InstaKibs  WinderTP  and Babscoole
description:
[b][size=6][center][u]Kibbles 'n Bits[/u][/center][/size][/b][b][size=4]The tasty treat that adds custom base objects and enhanced base building mechanics![/size][size=4][color=#00ff00]

[/color][color=#ff7700][u]SPECIAL NOTE[/u][/color] - Scaling of parts is enabled, [u]HOWEVER[/u] the game prevents any sort of snapping with scaled parts. This is a mechanic we haven't figured out how to bypass yet.
[/size][/b]
[b][u][size=4][color=#ff7700]IMPORTANT NOTES[/color]
[/size]
[/u][/b][b]ALWAYS BACKUP YOUR SAVES PRIOR TO ANY LARGE UPDATE. IF YOU SAVE WITHOUT THIS MOD OR ANY OTHER MODS THAT INCLUDE CUSTOM BASE PARTS, THOSE PARTS WILL BE REMOVED FROM YOUR SAVE FILE. THIS IS A MECHANIC THAT WE HAVE NO CONTROL OVER SADLY.[/b][b]

THERE WILL BE A CONFLICT WITH ANY OTHER BASE BUILDING MOD (IE GUMSK'S BASE BUILDING) THAT MODIFIES THE TABLES LISTED UNDER THE "DEFAULT TABLES MODIFIED" SECTION BELOW. YOU MUST MERGE ALL BASE BUILDING MODS IN GENERAL IF YOU WANT TO COMBINE SEVERAL AT ONCE.

[color=#00ffff][size=3]OPTIONS[/size][/color][/b]

There are [b]two [/b]versions of this mod:

[b]Kibbles 'n Bytes[/b] - includes all of my base building enhancements [b]without [/b]any custom base parts
[b]Kibbles 'n Bits[/b] - includes all of my base building enhancements as well as all of my custom parts listed below

[color=#ff7700]Please only choose one or other, not both![/color]

[color=#00ffff]LATEST UPDATES[/color][b][color=#00ff00]

[/color][color=#ffff00][size=5]CURRENT VERSION SUPPORTED -[/size][/color][color=#00ff00][size=5] Aquarius-[/size][/color][color=#ffff00][size=5] [/size][/color][color=#00ff00][size=5]5.11[/size][/color]

[b][color=#00ff00][size=3][b]Kibbles 'n Bits - [/b]5.11+[/size][/color]

[/b][/b]Rebuilt for Worlds
Restored some snapping capabilities for several of the Nexus parts
Reduced the metal-ness of the Nexus custom base parts to reduce glare
Fixed several base object placements

[b][b]Kibbles 'n Bits - [/b]4.60[/b]

Rebuilt for Orbital
Added all new colorable glow variants of the Nexus basic parts
Restored space POI items
Restored all custom basic parts
Restored custom Nexus pipes
Fixed several snapping and visual issues with the Nexus prefab tubes
Certain custom base items are now grouped together in the menu as variants

[b]Kibbles 'n Bytes - 4.60[/b]

Updated for Orbital
Updated LUA for use with AMUMSS
Note - Bytes and Bits versions are now synchronized

[b][color=#00ffff][size=3]CUSTOM BASE ITEMS[/size][/color][/b]

[b]241 [/b]custom base parts: 
﻿115 Nexus parts
﻿23 Nexus themed "basic" snappable parts
﻿﻿23 Nexus themed colorable glow "basic" snappable parts [color=#00ff00]-NEW[/color][color=#00ff00][size=1][b]
[/b][/size][/color] ﻿5 Nexus themed "tube" prefab parts
﻿7 Nexus themed colorable pipes
﻿21 Space spawns
﻿24 Custom colored lights
﻿23 Tron themed parts (modeled from metal parts)
﻿2 Chroma key floors and walls
﻿1 Space Station ship salvage station

[b]Nexus Parts[/b]

All Nexus parts have custom collisions to make removal and decoration easy.
All large Nexus props now collide with other base items properly! 
All large Nexus props are now colorable including glow!
Some parts offer snapping abilities (large props do not handle snapping well in general).
The largest props have a 1/10th scale version for flexibility.
Newly added Recipe Terminal.

[b]Nexus Basic Parts[/b]

23 custom glow Nexus themed parts, including curved floors, walls, and even railings! [color=#00ff00]-NEW[/color]
23 custom Nexus themed parts, including curved floors, walls, and even railings!
All parts are colorable, scalable, and have proper collisions.
All parts are fully snappable and use vanilla and or custom snap files for easy integration.
All floor panels have built-in hazard protection within a certain radius (thanks Gumsk for the refresher!)

[b]Nexus Prefab Tube Parts

[/b]4 new Nexus themed "tube" parts modeled after the underwater corridors.
1 custom Nexus door that has been scaled to fit the tubes properly, along with ramp attachments.
All parts are fully snappable and use vanilla and or custom snap files for easy integration.
﻿
[b]Space Spawns[/b]

All space spawns have been adjusted to be either level with the ground or elevated within reason.
Light strength has been lowered to not be so blinding with other surrounding features.

[b]Custom Colored Lights[/b]

There are 3 types of lights: spotlights, ambient lights, and wall lights.
The ambient lights have a wide spread of light useful for many things.
The spotlights are more concentrated (modeled after WinderTP's spotlight in Eucli-ea).
The wall lights have the least amount of light output and are useful for smaller areas.

[b]Tron Basic Parts[/b]

These are metal basic parts that have been re-skinned with a custom glow overlay.
These parts will be enhanced in the near future to minimize material stretching and to balance the overall glow.
These parts are now colorable (minus the glow) and have been reskinned.

[b]Chroma Key Parts[/b]

Simple chroma key walls and floors useful for many things.

[b][size=3][color=#00ffff]BASE BUILDING ENHANCEMENTS[/color]

[/size][/b]Turned off placeholder collision checking.
Enabled building of all base parts anywhere within the base radius (free standing doors, etc).
Removed planet, region, planet base, and freighter base limits.
Enabled freighter parts to be built on planets.
Disabled removal of attached decoration for vanilla base items.
Enabled scaling and rotation of base items.
Set all ghost count overrides to 1 to reduce snappoint ghosting.
Added all custom base decorations to the BASEBUILDINGCOSTSTABLE to prevent tear down issues.

[b][color=#00ffff][size=3]DEFAULT TABLES MODIFIED

[/size][/color][/b][u]Kibbles 'n Bits[/u]

[font=Arial][size=2]METADATA\REALITY\TABLES\BASEBUILDINGAUTOGENERATEDPARTSDATA.MBIN [/size][color=#00ff00][size=1]-NEW[/size][/color]
[size=2]﻿METADATA\REALITY\TABLES\BASEBUILDINGOBJECTSTABLE.MBIN[/size]
[/font][font=Arial][size=2]METADATA\REALITY\TABLES\BASEBUILDINGCOSTSTABLE.MBIN
[/size][/font][font=Arial][size=2]METADATA\REALITY\TABLES\NMS_REALITY_GCPRODUCTTABLE.MBIN
[/size][/font][font=Arial][size=2]METADATA\GAMESTATE\DEFAULTSAVEDATA.MBIN
[/size][/font][font=Arial][size=2]METADATA\GAMESTATE\DEFAULTSAVEDATACREATIVE.MBIN

[u]Kibbles 'n Bytes[/u]

METADATA\REALITY\TABLES\BASEBUILDINGOBJECTSTABLE.MBIN
[/size][/font]
[b][color=#00ffff][size=3]OTHER INFO[/size][/color][/b]

The BASEBUILDINGTABLE has been replaced by the BASEBUILDINGOBJECTSTABLE and I've reflected that above.


[b][size=5]Customisation/compatibility instructions with AMUMSS:[/size][/b]

[list=1]
[*]Keep "~~~KibblesNBits.pak" inside the MODS folder;
[*]Download the Kibbles N' Bits AMUMSS script;
[*]Open "+++++KibblesNBits_METADATA.lua";
[*]Regarding customisation available via the lua, see [b]AMUMSS Script exclusive customisation options[/b] below;
[*]To build a compatibility pak with other mods (see AMUMSS's page for details on building a mod), place all AMUMSS script of the conflicting mods in the ModScript folder;
[*]Run BUILDMOD.bat, press "Y" when asked by AMUMSS to create a merged pak, ~~~KibblesNBits_COMPATIBILITY.pak will be created.
[*]Happy times
[/list]Read the [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626]How to AMUMSS[/url] document and [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/1889]this guide[/url] for more information on how to merge mods with AMUMSS.﻿


[b][size=5]AMUMSS Script exclusive customisation options:

[/size][/b][list=1]
[*][b]POP_IN_RATE [/b]changes the rate of base object pop-in/pop-out, modify from [i]1-5[/i] (default is 2), see [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/articles/76]images in this article[/url]﻿ for example;
[*][b]FORCE_DECOR_TYPE [/b]forces all KnB object into one type of placement mode, read descriptions inside the lua script for more information default it empty (""); 

[*][b]OVERRIDE_INACTIVE [/b]turns on LOD models for all KnB objects, defaults is true. To disable LOD models, set to [i]false[/i];
[*][b]BUILD_ANYWHERE [/b]allows all KnB objects to be built outside base boundaries, default is false. To allow objects to be built anywhere on planet, set to [i]true[/i];
[*][b]MORE_VANILLA_FLOOR [/b]reverts the basic floors' advanced snapping capabilities to its vanilla form, default is false, to revert the floors to vanilla, set to [i]true[/i].
[/list]

files.changelog_html:
Updated for Expeditions 3.3+.
Added the space station ship station under the general tech menu.
Added 18 additional Nexus themed "basic" snappable parts.
Added 5 Nexus themed "tube" prefab parts.
Both Nexus basic and prefab parts are now colorable.
All large Nexus props are now colorable.
The Nexus vents are now snappable.
Adjusted the chroma basic parts to reduce glow bleed.
Fixed snapping and collision issues with the Nexus doors.
Fixed an issue with a Nexus landing pad being raised up.
Fixed an issue with the glow not being visible in one of the large Nexus props.
Removed the space spawn entities to fix an issue with cross-play crashing.
Adjusted all space spawns for better placement and VR support.
Reduced the brightness of the ambient lights by 25%.

This has all of the base building enhancements of Kibbles 'n Bits but without any custom base parts. Please either have this version or Kibbles 'n Bits loaded, not both. LUA included.

NOTE - Scaling of base parts sadly aren't snappable currently.
files.changelog_html:
Fixed compatibility error with Eucli-ea/Ultra Base Building where vanilla objects lose is snapping capabilities
Tweaked load order in AMUMSS

files.name: Kibbles n Bits
files.description: Updated with MBINCompiler v5.71.0.1 for NMS Build 145515 (5.72) from 12/06/2025, certain objects made invisible to improve stability
files.changelog_html:
Updated with MBINCompiler v5.71.0.1 for NMS Build 145254 (5.71) from 06/06/2025
certain objects made invisible to improve stability

1.0.0: Initial release - see overview for details.
1.0.1: Removed the Derelict "Heater" since Hello Games have added it themselves.
1.0.2: Fixed an issue with the Central Point Cage from not being visible
Fixed an issue with the Central Point Table from not being visible
1.0.3: Added the Nexus Egg Machine as a buildable part
Made all upcoming Quick Silver base items immediately available
1.1.0: Added the space station ship station under the general tech menu.
Added 18 additional Nexus themed "basic" snappable parts.
Both Nexus basic and prefab parts are now colorable.
All large Nexus props are now colorable.
The Nexus vents are now snappable.
Adjusted the chroma basic parts to reduce glow bleed.
Fixed snapping and collision issues with the Nexus doors.
Fixed an issue with a Nexus landing pad being raised up.
Fixed an issue with the glow not being visible in one of the large Nexus props.
Removed the space spawn entities to fix an issue with cross-play crashing.
Adjusted all space spawns for better placement and VR support.
Reduced the brightness of the ambient lights by 25%.
1.1.1: Added hazard protection to all Nexus basic custom floor panels.
Fixed an issue that allowed water in the Nexus custom prefab "tubes".
Removed the expedition base parts due to a bug prohibiting the collection of rewards from the Quick Silver shop.
1.2.0: Added 6 new Nexus decorations, including the new recipe terminal.
Increased model size of the ambient light models and centered the light distribution.
Re-skinned and enabled colorization of Tron basic parts (excluding the glow).
Made all upcoming Quick Silver base items immediately available (minus bubble tubes as they are not finished yet by HG).
1.3.0: Added 13 new Nexus themed custom parts:
All Nexus large props now have colorable glow
All Nexus large props now have proper collisions with other base items
Fixed an issue which prevented snapping of random custom pieces when combined with Eucli-ea
1.3.1: Custom Nexus pipes have been redone and are now contextual
Removed the legacy Nexus pipe and curved pipe
Corrected a collision issue with the Nexus Large Rings
Corrected the Nexus terminals from blocking interaction due to lack of power
Enhanced the Nexus Robo Arm Wall to remove transparency
3.84: KnB vanilla changes omitted for now
Converted METADATA file generation to use AMUMSS lua script, script released
AMUMSS script feature: FORCE_DECOR_TYPE will change every KnB object into the placement type of your choice, read the descriptions inside the script for more information
3.89: No change, other than renaming the .pak file
3.98: Updated to 3.98 to include value change fixes from the patch
3.98.1: Fixed compatibility error with Eucli-ea/Ultra Base Building where vanilla objects lose is snapping capabilities
4.08a: I accidentally injected a typo into the lua script for last version, leading to long game load time.  Should be corrected now.
4.36.1a: Temporary fix for three objects geometry files crashing the game.  Removed objects for now.
4.36.2: 3 crashing objects restored.  Many floor and wall objects now working again, Tube structures fixed.  Still ~17 objects to be fixed.
4.43: Update to 4.43, due to changes HG made in a file
4.60: Certain custom base items are now grouped together in the menu as variants
Added all new colorable glow variants of the Nexus basic parts
Fixed several snapping and visual issues with the Nexus prefab tubes.
4.64: Fixed several snapping issues with the large curved railings.
Adjusted LUA and mod to restore default (intended) placement of my custom base objects.
4.65: Fixed an issue with the Nexus pipe collision
Fixed a few shading issues with the Nexus prefab tubes
Fixed a few shading issues with the Nexus basic parts
5.01: Corrected the placement of several nexus building parts
Reduced "metalness" of the Nexus themed custom parts
Added additional snapping capabilities back to some of the Nexus parts
5.05: Added the Nexus Teleporter base part
5.12: Updated with MBINCompiler v5.55.0.1 for NMS Build 138017 (5.55) from 12/02/2025
5.13: Updated with MBINCompiler v5.71.0.1 for NMS Build 145254 (5.71) from 06/06/2025
certain objects made invisible to improve stability

name: 5.52 ZV4X Shadows Begone (plus FPS)
summary: Shadows is disabled. Lighting is not disabled. Bit more FPS with a good graphic. Now available merged with Antibloom version :)
author: ZV4X
description:
[center][size=6][color=#ff0000][b]UPDATED TO BE [color=#6aa84f]FULLY COMPATIBLE[/color] WITH WORLDS II UPDATE[/b][/color][b][color=#ff0000] [/color][/b][/size]





[size=5][b][color=#6666ff]INSTALLATION PROCESS:[/color]
[color=#3333ff]Move the folder from the archive to the \No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\MODS folder.[/color][/b]
[/size][color=#999999][size=2]...for old versions: unpack archive, after that copy "___ZV4X.Shadows.Begone.X.XX.pak" file to the \No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\PCBANKS\MODS[/size][/color]



[color=#f1c232]PIPELINEDEFERREDCUSTOMFSR2DEBUG was changed.
PIPELINEDEFERRED.BIN was changed.[/color]

[color=#444444]Someone in another mods talking about "mod is working but at half" or "it's all black, nothing to see, help".
-My mod is working optimal.[/color][/center]

files.name: ZV4X Shadows Begone
files.description:
Reworked to be compatible with Worlds II update.
To install: Move the folder from the archive to the \No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\MODS folder.

files.name: ZV4X Shadows Begone with AntiBloom
files.description:
Reworked to be compatible with Worlds II update.
To install: Move the folder from the archive to the \No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\MODS folder.

This version also disables Bloom effect.

name: Companion Pet Unlocker
summary: Allows the possibility to add almost any creature as a pet.
author: Mjjstral aka MetaIdea and Babscoole
description:
[color=#ff0000][b][size=4]Join our friendly and helpfull [size=5][url=https://discord.gg/AEXcap6]NMS Discord Community[/url][/size] with a special focus on ship seeds and creatures, modding, savegames, 3d printing, exploration and game tips and much more ;)[/size][/b][/color]
[size=5]
To tame air and sea creatures you must be on foot[/size]. That's a limitation of the game so wait till the air creature flys low or the sea creature is close to the shore. Or build a plattform in or above the water, marine shelter,  or a air tower.  See the videos on the video tab for successful techniques.

FAQ:
[list]
[*]unlocked creatures can lay eggs[/*]
[*]unlocked creatures can be milked[/*]
[*]More creatures can now be ridden[/*]
[*]other players can see them too[/*]
[/list]

[b][size=6]Install:  Extract archive contents to GAMEDATA\MODS[/size]

Mod Description:

[/b]15 Nov 2022:  Now includes reduction of pet egg times

16 Feb 2022:  Tutorial added on Video Tab for capturing/taming Flying Lizards, courtesy of Zelutherian.

8 July 2021:  v3.53a.  Adds the ability to ride some creatures that couldn't be ridden before, thanks to Corvus
Now Rideable:
[list]
[*]FISH[/*]
[*]FISHFLOCK[/*]
[*]SWIMCOW[/*]
[*]SWIMRODENT[/*]
[*]JELLYFISH[/*]

[*]FIENDFISHSMALL[/*]
[*]FLOATER[/*]

[/list]
Allows the possibility to add almost any creature as a pet.

files.changelog_html: Added DEEPFISHLARGE

3.42a: 5 of the creature types in the mod already had the pet code in them, potentially causing issues.  Implemented fix.
3.53a: Adds the ability to ride some creatures that couldn't be ridden before, thanks to Corvus
3.53b: Fixed issue with swimrodent and added riding robots
3.62: Update for Frontiers.  Based on a single report that someone had gotten a Sandworm as a pet without the game crashing, have re-enabled sandworms.  See how that goes.
3.70: Updated to 3.70.  Removed Sandworm, as it is now officially supported as Pet Tamable by HG.
3.71: Updated to 3.71.  Removed several creatures from MOD where HG has added Pet code for them in vanilla.  Report that Flying Snake cannot currently be added as pet (no E interaction menu).  Used AMUMSS 3.9.4W to create .pak file.
3.72: Turns out the "sandworm" allowed as a pet in vanilla for Emergence is actually a flying snake.  Adding REAL sandworm back in.
3.81: Removed GCCreatureGlobal entry.  Added SentinelMech...maybe it'll work, maybe not.
3.88: Mystery of the flying lizards solved.
4.03a: Emergency fix.  Previous 4.x versions weren't working correctly, due to a bug in my AMUMSS install.  Created the mod incorrectly from the lua script.
4.06a: Significant feature update.  Pet egg time reduction code added, fixed riding joint for flying lizards, and big changes surrounding creature interaction distance.  Also slimmed the LUA a little via function/tables/loops.  Big thanks go out to Silent369, who is now credited as a mod Contributor.  Be aware the mod now makes multiple changes to GCCREATUREGLOBALS.MBIN.
4.13: Update for 4.13.  Added prompt to lua, when run through AMUMSS, to not add pet egg timer changes.
4.40: Update to 4.40 Echoes.  Refactoring to Lua script.
5.58: Update for NMS 5.58, Worlds Pt II.  101 files getting the EXML treatment, fingers crossed.
5.70: Update for NMS 5.70, Beacon.  Added Squid

name: Consumerism - Expedition Twitch and Community reward unlocker
summary: Unlocks Expedition, Twitch, and Community Rewards for purchase in the Quicksilver shop
author: WinderTP
description:
[center][size=5]HI, WINDERTP HERE FOR[/size]
[font=Verdana][size=6][b][u]CONSUMERISM™

[/u][/b][/size][/font][size=5]THE EASIEST WAY TO BUY, BUY, BUY YOUR WAY TO SUCCESS

[/size][size=4][b]CONSUMERISM™[/b] LETS YOU BUY ANYTHING, EVERYTHING* IN [b]THE QUICKSILVER SHOP[/b]

ARE YOU A PERSON WHO DOESN'T CONTRIBUTE TO THE COMMUNITY
BUT WANTS TO REAP THE REWARDS BEFORE ANYONE ELSE?
GET [b]CONSUMERISM™[/b] TODAY AND GET A HEAD START[/size]
[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/2062/2062-1630866855-250424957.jpeg[/img]﻿

[size=4]ARE YOU A PERSON WHO DOESN'T HAVE THE TIME TO WATCH CONTENT CREATORS
BUT HAVE THE CAPITAL TO BANKRUPT THE QUICKSILVER SYNTHESIS COMPANION?
GET [b]CONSUMERISM™[/b] TODAY AND FEEL GOOD ABOUT YOURSELF[/size]
[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/2062/2062-1630866865-1581207615.jpeg[/img]﻿

[size=4]ARE YOU A PERSON WHO DOESN'T DRIVE A DELOREAN
AND THEREFORE CANNOT GO BACK IN TIME TO FINISH A TIMED EVENT
THAT NOT EVERYBODY HAS THE TIME TO FINISH IN A TIMELY MANNER AND YOU WERE TOO OUTATIME TO DO?
GET [b]CONSUMERISM™[/b] TODAY AND BUY ALL THE TITLES YOU COULD ASK FOR[/size]
[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/1634/images/2062/2062-1630866874-380858041.jpeg[/img]﻿

[size=6]EVEN SPACE IS CORRUPTED BY CAPITALISM[/size]
[size=4]GET [b]CONSUMERISM™[/b] TODAY FOR FREE
AND START SPENDING YOUR HARD-EARNED QUICKSILVER
[/size](Lua available for auto-update and adding more products to the Quicksilver shop)[/center]
[center][url=https://nomanssky.fandom.com/wiki/Mods#Installing_Mods][size=6][b]Installing Mods Manually[/b][/size][/url]﻿﻿[/center][size=6][center][url=https://stepmodifications.org/wiki/NoMansSky:Tutorials/HowToAMUMSS]How To merge with AMUMSS[/url]
[url=https://stepmodifications.org/wiki/NoMansSky:Tutorials/Merging_two_mods]How to merge without AMUMSS[/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626]How to use AMUMSS (pdf)[/url][/center][/size]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/957][size=5][b][center]Click here to go to AMUMSS's Nexus mod page.[/center][/b][/size][/url]

[font=Times New Roman][size=1]*Not actually everything, just things that are already in the Quicksilver shop, most Twitch rewards and most Expedition rewards
[/size][/font]
[size=4][u][b]ALL EXML files are configured to be maximally compatible with other mods and require no merging via AMUMSS[/b][/u][/size]
Edits METADATA\REALITY\TABLES\PURCHASEABLESPECIALS.EXML

Thumbnail overlay originally by [url=https://www.reddit.com/r/starterpacks/comments/ahqp8p/end_of_an_infomercial_starter_pack/]brerkmgher[/url]

name: Clouds Sun and Fog
summary: Realtime (NMS time) Day Length (Optional), Better Detailed Clouds, Slower Cloud Animation, Sun Properties and Planet Fog Adjustments and LOD Adjustments, Optional Lens Dirt Removal.
author: Silent369
description:
[b][size=6][center]Clouds Sun and Fog v5.8[/center][/size][/b]
[quote][size=3][b]Not being satisfied with the current implementation of several environmental behaviours / effects this mod aims to improve some of these features.[/b]

[b]** NOTE[/b]: All LOD settings have been restored into the main mod again, it is easier to maintain this way.

If recompiling with AMUMSS you can now control the Fog Strength and Fog Height (valid ranges: 0.0-1.0) for Clouds Sun And Fog
[/size][/quote][size=3]
Day Length can be altered to suit your own preferences. [b][u]The default is 30 Minutes[/u][/b] (NMS Time) day/night cycle. There are now various versions available with different time ratios for Day Length as shown below.

[b]Time Ratios Available:[/b]
[/size][quote][size=3]30Mn =  1800 (game/script default)
01Hr  =  3600
03Hr  = 10800
06Hr  = 21600
09Hr = 32400
12Hr  = 43200
24Hr  = 86400
48 Hr = 172800[/size][/quote][size=3]
[b]Clouds[/b] have been overhauled for more coverage and detail, animation speed and colour bias.
[b]Fog[/b] has been greatly reduced and adjusted so that it now should only appear near the far horizon.
[b]Sun [/b]properties have been altered for [b]brightness[/b], [b]saturation[/b], [b]MIE factor[/b] and [b]fixes sun clamp angle[/b].
[b]Sun Size[/b] in Space is reduced to give the illusion of it being a great distance away rather than very close. 
[b]Bloom[/b] has been disabled via PIPELINES files.

Where possible / appropriate I have kept all original values for reference.

Modifies the following:
[/size][quote][u][size=3]GCGRAPHICSGLOBALS.GLOBAL.MBIN
GCENVIRONMENTGLOBALS.GLOBAL.MBIN
GCSKYGLOBALS.GLOBALS.MBIN
PIPELINES\PIPELINEDEFERRED.BIN
[u]PIPELINES\PIPELINEDEFERREDVR.BIN[/u][/size][/u][/quote][size=3]
[b]Installing No Man's Sky Mods[/b] (Steam).[/size]

[quote][b][size=3]Installing No Man's Sky Mods[/size][/b] (Steam).

[b][u]Default Steam installation location:[/u][/b]
C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\MODS

[b][u]Custom Steam installation location:[/u][/b]
E:\Steam\steamapps\common\No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\MODS

1. Browse to the "No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA" folder.
2. Ensure there is a folder named MODS (Uppercase!) within, if not create it!
3. Place the <modname> folder into the GAMEDATA\MODS folder.
4. Delete the file: GCMODSETTINGS.MXML from No Man's Sky\Binaries\SETTINGS
(It will be auto-regenerated with a new load order based on the mods you have)

If done correctly the game will display a MOD Warning Page: [u]Click to continue[/u].
You should now be presented with your save games list to choose from to start playing.[/quote]
Made with [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/957][i]AMUMSS[/i][/url]. Check my other [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/users/122177308?tab=user+files]MODS[/url].
Lua's included in the main download. ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

name: ALFA_NoGameNoLive
summary:
Rebalance Mod
-Economy -Shop -Damage -AI -Ship etc...
Mod for a new game ( Preferably )
author: Dollex
description:
[size=3][u][b]Ver. Beta-0.4:[/b][/u]
The balance in multitools has been changed.
The rank of starships, as well as the increase in the inventory of starships is made for units.
Other minor edits...

[u]Ver. Beta-0.3:[/u]
[size=3]The star space has been changed:
Pirates now pose a threat, and a random reward in the form of a unit of currency has been added for defeating them. 
Asteroids have undergone minor changes.
Systems and galaxies are also edited... In this regard, after launching with this update, the space station in your system may disappear, therefore, before updating, it is advisable that a hyperdrive be installed on the spaceship. ( This will also be edited and refined in the future )[/size][size=1]
P.S Removed garbage that was accidentally included in the mod archive[/size]

[u][b]Ver. Beta-0.2A:[/b][/u]
The influence of the environment on protection from harmful factors has been adjusted.
The balance in the initial technologies of the multitool for resource extraction has been changed
Shields protecting against harmful factors have also been changed, they also increase resistance to harmful factors, but their duration has been shortened.
The recipe for the energy suit charging item has been changed.

[u][b]Ver. Beta-0.2:[/b][/u]
Adjustment of the cost of starships.
Possible bonus parameters of starships depending on their class and rank
are changed. The influence of the environment on protection from harmful factors has been changed.
Bug fixes have been made...
Tested the ability to play in [b]survival mode - OK[/b]. [u][b][[/b][/u]Survival mode of increased difficulty[b]] But it's worth trying a new game ( It's really survival)[/b]
Changed the reward for saving a freighter

[u][b]Ver. Beta-0.1:[/b][/u]
Teleports are disabled.
Prices for products and resources vary depending on the economy.
Damage sources increase at least 1.5 times.
Changed prices for spaceships, frigates and freighters. 
The ground battles and battles in space have undergone significant and not significant changes
[/size]and other...

To install, move the contents of the archive to "No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\PCBANKS\mods\..." 
and rename DISABLEMODS.TXT -> ENABLEMODS.TXT in the directory "No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\PCBANKS" 
to delete, you must delete ALFA_NoGameNoLive.pak from "No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\PCBANKS\mods\..."
The mod will be finalized, so if you find any problems and oddities, please let us know so that such inconveniences are corrected in subsequent updates.


for No Man's Sky Endurance ver.
Compatibility has not been tested. there is no lua, because the mod will be updated every 3 days or whenever possible.

name: Powerful Terrain Editor
summary: This mod multiply the amount of resources you get from using the terrain editor.
author: Belcazzi49
description:
This mod (my first one, !) allows you to gather mineral resources faster when using the terrain manipulator. It comes in 5 different flavors :

- X2 resources
- X5 resources
- X10 resources
- X100 resources
- X1000 resources (basically : Click once and be rich mode" !)

The files include the .lua scripts if you want to tweak them at your conveniance or merge them with others mods that use GCGAMEPLAYGLOBALS.GLOBAL.EXML

Fully Working on NMS 4.0

name: Derelict Speed Increase
summary: For those in need of speed inside derelicts.
author: jackty89
description:
[size=5][b]Description[/b][/size]

[size=4]Bored of being bound to slow walking or running inside derelicts?
Well than this mod is for you.
Lua and CS (NMSMB) scripts are available as seperate downloads.[/size]

[line]
[size=5][b]Technical Details[/b][/size]

[size=4][b]Files Changed[/b][/size]
[quote]
[list]
[*]GCPLAYERGLOBALS.GLOBAL.MBIN
[/list][/quote]
[size=4][b]Mod Format Explantion[/b][/size]
[quote]
[b]EXML Format:[/b] [u]Partial edit[/u], only touches the lines/values that need editing. Very low chance of conflicts with other mods (unless they edit the same values). Can be edited easily by user using an editor like Notepad ++/VSCODE/... .
[b]MBIN Format:[/b] [u]Full File Replacement[/u]. High chance of conflict, comparable with old pak format (pre. WORLDS). Can be used together with EXML, MBIN mods are loaded first so changes in EXML mods that edit the same file will still be applied. Other MBIN mods that change the same file follow the FIFO principle, merging is highly recommended with MBIN mods (see How To).
[b]Lua Format:[/b] Script file used by AMUMSS to generate the mod or used for merging with this tool.
[b]CS Format:[/b] Script file used by NMSMB to generate the mod or used for merging with this tool.[/quote]
[size=4][b]Installation[/b][/size]
[quote]
[b]Install path:[/b] ...\No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\MODS
[b]How to install:[/b] Recommend installation is manual, vortex can be used but some mods contain multiple versions so user might need to delete files/folders. Common steps, download mod, extract the file(s) from archived file and copy to the install path.[/quote]
[line]
[size=5][b]Mod Details[/b][/size]
[quote]
[b][u]Mod Format:[/u][/b] Full EXML

[b][u]What changes[/u][/b]
[list]
[*]This mod changes the walking and running speed to that of the what you would have normally outside derelicts
[/list]
[b][u]Scirpt Options[/u][/b]
[list]
[*]These speed can be further tweaked by changing the muliplier: SpeedMultiplier (lua and NMSMB)
[*]The lua supports GUIF (user input)
[/list][/quote]
[line]
[size=5][b]Extra information[/b][/size]

[size=4][b]Interesting links[/b][/size]
[quote]
[b]Modding Discord:[/b] [url=https://discord.com/invite/22ZAU9H]Discord[/url]
[b]Step WIKI:[/b] [url=https://stepmodifications.org/wiki/NoMansSky]Step WIKI[/url][/quote]
[size=4][b]How To Guides[/b][/size]
[quote]
[url=https://stepmodifications.org/wiki/NoMansSky:Tutorials/HowToAMUMSS]Quick Guide: How To merge with AMUMSS[/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2521]Extensive Guide: How to use NMSMB (pdf)[/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2626]Extensive Guide: How to use AMUMSS (pdf)[/url][/quote]

name: Bonus for resources  Fraktal
summary: When extracting resources , it provides a significant bonus ...
author: Tuz1971
description:
If you are tired of constantly running around and looking for resources, extracting, for example, from a stone - 20 or from a tree - 30-40 units, then this mod is for you from a tree - 4000 :))) There are up to 25,000- 30,000 from large structures ...
If the cell is not forced - 5000%, and if forced, then 6275%...
Geological cannon - very effectively collects all the resources in the explosion area if you use a mod that removes restrictions on the installation of technologies, it is super effective, for example, I have 11 modules of class "S" with resources, I have no problems with a lot of resources From one shot, and then you need to put a lot of shields in a suit otherwise it will kill ...
In general, try...

name: Start with TARDIS
summary:
Startup script which turns your starting ship into a TARDIS, gives you the Emeril engine trail for free, and skips the tutorial.

Requires Custom Ships. New Ship Trail Colors is recommended.
author: RemingtonRyder
description:
[color=#00ff00][color=#e69138]Not currently working with No Man's Sky WORLDS PT 2 version 5.54 (February 5, 2025)[/color][/color]

This startup script will turn your starting ship into a TARDIS, give you the Emeril engine trail for free, and skip the tutorial.

Requires Custom Ships. New Ship Trail Colors is recommended.

When using the Custom Ships mod, I carefully browsed the list of custom ships to see which one would work best for a new playthrough, and then after a split second I chose the 1950s police box aka the TARDIS. So, I thought to myself, why not save myself having to go into the save editor each time?

So, that's why this mod exists.

Please remember to check and see if Custom Ships and New Ship Trail Colors have updated.

name: Dropship Restoration
summary: A collection of minor tweaks to the Dropship
author: lMonk
description:
[color=#BF9000][b][size=5]Dropship Restoration 1.31[/size][/b][/color]

[color=#BF9000][size=4][b]Description[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3]A collection of minor tweaks to the Dropship.
- decal placements improvements.
- Move guns below the cockpit (to match the interior firing placement).
- dimmer interior light.
- disable tail subwing_A bugged anim.
- subwing_F dim lights.
- LOD improvement.

⚠️ Overwrite mod - unlikely to work well with other Overwrite mods
[/size]
[color=#BF9000][size=4][b]Install[/b][/size][/color]
[size=3][size=3]Unzip the mod and place the full folder into [color=#f4cccc]{NMS Install Folder}\GAMEDATA\MODS[/color] (not PCBANKS). Create MODS folder if necessary.
[size=3]If the mod(s) still doesn't work, delete the mod settings file [color=#f4cccc]{NMS Install Folder}\Binaries\SETTINGS\GCMODSETTINGS.MXML[/color] and try again.[/size][/size]
◈ The LUA isn't used by the game directly. It's an aid for merging with other mods, using AMUMSS, or modding yourself. If you don't do either, you can safely ignore it.[/size]

[color=#BF9000][size=4][b]Change-log[/b][/size][/color]

name: Gourmet Flavours
summary: Replaces the 10 vanilla rewards for eating food with 54 fully custom ones.
author: CorpInc15
description:
Tired of bread being the best food for endless jetpack boosting? Seeking a reason to prepare complex food? This mod has you covered! All food rewards have been redesigned and rebalanced to each give each food type a unique purpose and incentivize complex cooking.

Compatible with Aquarius (version 5.11).
Lua script included for use with [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/957]AMUMSS[/url].
Credit to [url=http://next.nexusmods.com/profile/fluxxdog/mods?gameId=1634]fluxxdog[/url] for the wonderful [url=http://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/2462]Gourmet Food Descriptions[/url] mod, which this mod is based on.

[u][size=5]New Rewards[/size][/u]

In addition to rebalancing the nutritional benefits common to all foods (life support recharge, core health restoration, etc.), this mod gives following categories of foods unique rewards:

[list]
[*][size=2][color=#00ff00]H﻿﻿orrific food[/color] sometimes gives units, but has a chance of summoning Biological Horrors[/size]
[*][size=2][color=#ff0000]Anomalous food[/color] can reveal atlas words, but has a chance of attract Sentinel forces[/size]
[*][size=2][color=#9900ff]Stellar food[/color] often contain nanites, but can also summon storms[/size]
[*][color=#ead1dc][size=2]Raw food[/size][/color][size=2] causes damage when consumed[/size]
[*][size=2]Milk products restore shield strength[/size]
[*][color=#ea9999][size=2]Meat dishes[/size][/color][color=#f4f4f4][size=2] restore an exceptional amount of life support power[/size][/color]
[*][color=#b6d7a8][size=2]Plant dishes[/size][/color][color=#f4f4f4][size=2] restore an exceptional amount of hazard protection[/size][/color]
[*][color=#cfe2f3][size=2]Ice cream [/size][/color][color=#f4f4f4][size=2]provides shield strength and a jetpack boost[/size][/color]
[*][color=#dbb0a7][size=2]Doughnuts[/size][/color][color=#f4f4f4][size=2] offer an exceptionally strong jetpack boost[/size][/color]
[*][color=#c9daf8][size=2]Cakes [/size][/color][color=#f4f4f4][size=2]provide a long lasting sprint boost[/size][/color]
[/list]
Not all rewards are listed here, so get out there, and get cooking! 

[u][size=5]Modified Files[/size]
[/u][list]
[*]METADATA\REALITY\TABLES\CONSUMABLEITEMTABLE.MBIN
[*]METADATA\REALITY\TABLES\REWARDTABLE.MBIN
[*]LANGUAGE\NMS_LOC4_ENGLISH.MBIN
[*]LANGUAGE\NMS_LOC5_ENGLISH.MBIN
[*]LANGUAGE\NMS_LOC6_ENGLISH.MBIN
[/list]

name: Communism - A Consumerism AddOn
summary: Makes every Expedition and Twitch item added by Consumerism FREE OF CHARGE.
author: Syzzle
description:
[center]That's right Comrade, are you also tired of the capitalism devils dictating what you can or can not have? If you are then you can join us, this little trick can help you achieve everything you desire and the best part is that you will be able to do that FOR FREE!
You've heard it right, FOR FREE!

[size=5][b]THERE IS NO CATCH![/b][/size]

If you want to bask in this red glory then just install our [i]thing[/i] on your game, and as you probably have guessed, this thing is also FREE!

If you still not believe in what we're saying, then don't believe on us, believe on them who believes on us.

[img]https://i.imgur.com/y3JelQA.png[/img]﻿﻿[/center][center]﻿[/center][center][size=4][b]Convinced now?[/b][/size]
Then join us in this WAR against CAPITALISM, to do it is pretty simple, download our program and follow the instructions below to activate it.[/center]
[quote][size=5][b]Installing Mods[/b][/size]
Follow these steps to install mods.

[list=1]
[*]Browse to [b]No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA[/b].
[*]Create a folder named [b]MODS[/b].
[*]Place the [b]mod folder containing the loose files into the MODS folder[/b] (they have to be in the MODS folder itself and not in any subfolder, and no loose files should be inside the MODS folder).
[*]Start the game and wait for the splash screen to disappear.
[*]If done correctly [b]the[/b] [b]game will display a[/b] [b]warning[/b] just before displaying the selection of saved games.
[/list][/quote][center]The warning will look like [url=https://i.imgur.com/zTEEFwI.jpeg]this[/url].[/center]

[center][img]https://i.imgur.com/kVHngZn.png[/img]﻿[/center]

files.name: Communism - Free Void Egg
files.description: Glory to the collective! The Void Egg, a tool for the people, is now liberated from the capitalist yoke of price and is rightfully free. For it is not an item of decadent, bourgeois expeditions, but a common resource for all.
files.changelog_html: Due to a change in policy, the Void Egg is now considered an item of capitalist luxury and must be bought with a price, rather than being given freely to the people.

files.name: Communism
files.description: Here is your passport, Comrade.
files.changelog_html: Attention, comrades! The Eggs from the glorious Xeno Arena update have been liberated from the greedy grip of the bourgeoisie! They have been seized and redistributed to the proletariat, and now belong to the people at absolutely no cost. Glory to the collective!

6.0: Received the intel from Agent 6.0 with the new armaments for our cause.
6.03: Due to a change in policy, the Void Egg is now considered an item of capitalist luxury and must be bought with a price, rather than being given freely to the people.
6.04: Comrades,  In our collective struggle for a more just universe, we have liberated some of the Expedition Ships from the oppressive burden of Nanites. The People's Fleet is now more accessible to all who contribute to the common good!
6.05: Comrade, the Deadeye Cannon is now, by the will of the people and for the glory of the collective, a resource liberated from the chains of capitalist exchange! It is our cannon, and its use is free for all who serve the cause!
6.10: Comrades! The new resources secured by the victorious Breach expedition are now property of the collective and have been added to the people's inventory!
6.20: Comrades! The glorious Remnant expedition has arrived with spoils for the Collective! The Party has authorized the distribution of superior truck modifications for the proletariat. Best of all, these assets have been liberated from the chains of currency and they are available to all loyal workers at zero cost! Glory to the State!
6.32: Attention, comrades! The Eggs from the glorious Xeno Arena update have been liberated from the greedy grip of the bourgeoisie! They have been seized and redistributed to the proletariat, and now belong to the people at absolutely no cost. Glory to the collective!

name: DUD'S - Terra Colors
summary: More color variety for planet terrain.
author: jasondude7116
description:
[color=#ffff00]**[/color][size=4][b][url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/jasondude7116/mods?sortBy=name]Click HERE for links to all DUD'S mods[/url]﻿[/b][/size][color=#ffff00]**

[/color]Compatible with NMS v6.24

[color=#00ffff][size=4][b]Terra Colors[/b]
[/size][/color]v624-A

Adds custom tile color palette choices to all biomes.
These are not additional colors. They are control sets for different ground elements. (Grass, snow, dirt, rock, sand, etc.)
Vanilla NMS uses 1 color palette per each ground element/tile.
This gives some ground elements multiple palette choices.


[b][color=#ffff00]Mod Installation:
[/color][/b]1. Add mod folder inside .zip file to:
C:/(NMS installation folder)/No Man's Sky/GAMEDATA/MODS folder.
2. Delete C:/(NMS installation folder)/No Man's Sky/Binaries/SETTINGS/GCMODSETTINGS.MXML
3. Enjoy.

name: Space Eggs Remixed
summary: Shooting space eggs now has a chance to drop Spawning Sacs and Psychonic Eggs.
author: Raevyxn and Effex
description:
[b][size=4]DESCRIPTION
[/size][/b]
"Space Eggs Remixed" brings storage augmentation and specialty tech to the loot table for space eggs. Pulse around in space, and if your deep space encounter is the floating void egg, shooting it now has the chance to drop a Spawning Sac or a Psychonic Egg (in addition to the standard chance to drop other living ship tech upgrades). 

No Man's Sky currently locks living ship storage & specialty tech behind needing a freighter + organic frigate + successful fleet mission = a chance to get spawning sacs and/or psychonic eggs. This mod removes that barrier. [b]


[b]Related Mods[/b]
- [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/nomanssky/mods/4121]All Ships See Space Eggs[/url]:[/b] Allows you to encounter space eggs while piloting any ship type.[b]


***


[size=4]FOUR VERSIONS OF THIS MOD [i](Download only ONE version)[/i]
[/size]
- Balanced [/b][i](All_14perc)[/i]: This is the default download. A balanced version, where all loot from space eggs has the same 14% drop rate.

[b]- Prioritized Specials[/b] [i](Specials_49perc)[/i]: This version prioritizes the newly added loot. Spawning Sacs and Psychonic Eggs each have a 49% drop rate, while the five tech upgrade modules each have a <1% drop rate.

[b]- Rare Specials[/b] ([i]Specials_5perc)[/i]: This version makes the newly added loot a rare drop (since they are difficult to get by default in game). Spawning Sacs and Psychonic Eggs each have a 5% drop rate, while the five tech upgrades each have an 18% drop rate.

[b]- Prioritized Storage[/b] [i](Storage_94perc)[/i]: This version prioritizes the Spawning Sac drop rate (94%) and deprioritizes all other drops (1%).
[b]
[/b]
* * *


[b][size=4]FAQ
[/size][/b]
[b]Q[/b]:[b] How often will I receive Spawning Sacs and/or Psychonic Eggs when I shoot down space eggs?[/b]
[b]A[/b]: In the default mod (Balanced version), you'll have a 1-in-7 chance to receive a Spawning Sac, and a 1-in-7 chance to receive a Psychonic Egg.
[i]Math: The standard, unmodded loot table has 5 items, and normally each item has a 20% drop rate (1 item/5 items = 0.20 = 20%). With 7 items in this modded loot table, each item now has a [b]14% drop rate[/b] (1 item/7 items = 0.14 = 14%). [/i]
[b]
Q: I'd prefer it if the Spawning Sacs and Psychonic Eggs had an even lower drop rate compared to the tech upgrade modules. How can I do that?[/b]
[b]A: [/b]If you'd like to set these two items to be more rare, lower the PercentageChance for both the ALIEN_INV_TOKEN and the ALIEN_TECHBOX items.
For the [b]5% drop rate[/b] (available to download), PercentageChance for both items are set to 28 (instead of 100). [i]Math: 28/556 = 0.05 = 5%. The 556 comes from having the 5 original items set with a weight of 100, and the two new items set with a weight of 28 (so 100x5 + 28 + 28 = 556).[/i]
For a [b]2% drop rate[/b], set them both to 11 (instead of 100). [i]Math: 11/522 = 0.021 = 2.1%.[/i]


* * *


[b][size=4]INSTALLATION INSTRUCTIONS
[/size][/b]
Installing No Man's Sky Mods (Steam):

Default Steam installation location:
C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\[b]No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA\MODS[/b]
﻿﻿﻿
1. Navigate to the "No Man's Sky\GAMEDATA" folder.
﻿﻿﻿2. Ensure there is a folder named "MODS" within. If there is no MODS folder here, then create it. (Must be uppercase: MODS)
﻿﻿﻿3. Place the extracted folder for this mod into the GAMEDATA\MODS folder.
﻿﻿﻿4. Navigate back up to No Man's Sky\Binaries\SETTINGS, and delete the file
called "GCMODSETTINGS.MXML". (It will be auto-regenerated with a new
load order based on the mods you have.)

If done correctly the game will display a "MOD Warning" page when you launch it. Click to continue.
You should now be presented with your save games list to choose from to start playing.
